zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Harcourt於1256年出生在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特時，他的父親William de Harcourt30歲，母親Hilary Hastings19歲。他和Margaret Beke至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1293年3月1日在他的家鄉享年37歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Harcourt was born in 1256, in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, his father, William de Harcourt, was 30 and his mother, Hilary Hastings, was 19. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Margaret Beke. He died on 1 March 1293, in his hometown, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Harcourt est né en 1256, à Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Harcourt, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Hilary Hastings, avait 19 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Margaret Beke. Il est décédé le 1 mars 1293, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 37 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Harcourtが1256年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン ハーコウルトで生まれた時，父William de Harcourtは30歳，母Hilary Hastingsは19歳であった。彼はMargaret Bekeとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1293年3月1日に故郷で37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Harcourt(이)가 1256년 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Harcourt(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Hilary Hastings(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Margaret Beke(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1293년 3월 1일 그의 고향에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Harcourt родился в 1256 г. в местности стантон харкоурт, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Harcourt было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Hilary Hastings, 19. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Margaret Beke. Он умер 1 марта 1293 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 37 лет.
